content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL p RACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Michael Millman 
P.O. Box 64637 
2100 Sawtelle Boulevard, Suite 105 
Los Angeles, CA 90064 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-14-029 
Dear Mr. Millman: 
March 25, 2014 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 
After a review of your request, we are offering informal assistance as the facts presented 
are too vague to render formal advice.2 Because your questions are general in nature and not 
limited to a specific governmental decision, we are treating your request as one for informal 
assistance. 3 
You ask several general questions regarding lobbyists potentially arranging or advising· 
on political campaign contributions. Enclosed is the Boatwright Advice Letter, A-09-161 and the 
Leonard Advice Letter, 1-93-170, which provide general guidance in this area. I have also 
enclosed a copy of the FPPC's Lobbying Disclosure Manual for your reference. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 9 JO 14. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission arc contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references arc to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
2 While the Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to a person whose duties under the Act 
arc in question (Section 83 I 14(b); Regulation I 8329(a)), the Commission may decline to provide advice if a 
response relates to past conduct or if the facts presented are insufficient or too vague to render specific informal 
assistance. (Regulation I 8329(b)(8)(A) and (c)(4)(A) and (F)). In this instance, we have elected to provide general 
guidelines rather than declining your request for advice. 
3 Informal assistance docs not provide the Vequestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal 
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation I 8329(c)(3).) 


File No. 1-14-029 
Page No. 2 
We hope you find these materials helpful. Please contact us in the future should you 
have a request for advice regarding a specific governmental decision. 
Sincerely, 
ER:jgl

sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked general questions regarding whether lobbyists may arrange or advise on political campaign contributions under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that the facts presented were too vague to render formal advice and offered informal assistance instead, directing the requester to relevant prior advice letters (Boatwright A-09-161 and Leonard 1-93-170) and the FPPC's Lobbying Disclosure Manual for general guidance.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination/informal assistance letter without explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. The substantive inquiry is embedded in the body text. No formal advice was rendered due to vagueness of facts and general nature of questions. Document includes extensive footnotes regarding statutory authority and the distinction between formal and informal assistance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester asked general questions regarding whether lobbyists may arrange or advise on political campaign contributions under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC determined that the facts presented were too vague to render formal advice and offered informal assistance instead, directing the requester to relevant prior advice letters (Boatwright A-09-161 and Leonard 1-93-170) and the FPPC's Lobbying Disclosure Manual for general guidance.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to Michael Millman's request for advice regarding lobbyist involvement in campaign contributions by offering informal assistance with references to prior guidance, as the questions presented were too general and vague for formal written advice.
